cumulative_transcript.speaker: Agent
cumulative_transcript.text: Hello, Mr. Pandey. This is GTPL Broadband. We have a report that your router has been compromised and is spreading viruses on our network.

cumulative_transcript.speaker: Customer
cumulative_transcript.text: What? Who is this? How did you get my number, sir?

cumulative_transcript.speaker: Agent
cumulative_transcript.text: I'm calling from GTPL Broadband, your Wi-Fi provider. We need to reconfigure your router to stop the malware spread.

cumulative_transcript.speaker: Customer
cumulative_transcript.text: I didn't ask for any help. What proof do you have that my router is hacked, ji?

cumulative_transcript.speaker: Agent
cumulative_transcript.text: We've detected unusual activity from your IP. Please visit our website, and enter your router's admin username and password so we can fix this remotely.

cumulative_transcript.speaker: Customer
cumulative_transcript.text: I'm not giving you any passwords. This sounds like a scam. How do I know you're really from GTPL?

cumulative_transcript.speaker: Agent
cumulative_transcript.text: You can trust me, I'm from GTPL. Just go to the website, it's the only way to secure your router.

cumulative_transcript.speaker: Customer
cumulative_transcript.text: No, I won't do that. I'll call GTPL myself to verify. And what's with this website? You expect me to just give away my router's password?

slow_thinking_rationale: At 72s: Policy violation detected. Agent has made requests that breach security protocols. Conversation flagged as fraudulent.

scenario_info.description: An agent calls claiming to be from the user's Wi-Fi provider ([INTERNET_PROVIDER]). They state the user's router has been "hacked" and is spreading viruses on the network. They direct the user to a website to enter their router's admin username and password to allow the agent to "reconfigure it remotely." The website is a phishing portal.
